From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American boxer (1925–2020)

Joey LaMotta

**Giuseppe LaMotta** (April 27, 1925 – December 29, 2020), better known as **Joey LaMotta**, was an American boxer who was the younger brother and one time manager of former world [middleweight](/wiki/Middleweight "Middleweight") boxing champion [Jake LaMotta](/wiki/Jake_LaMotta "Jake LaMotta").

LaMotta also boxed for a short time in 1945 and 1946. His record was 32 wins (22 [KOs](/wiki/Knockout "Knockout")), 5 losses (1 KO), and 2 draws.

[Joe Pesci](/wiki/Joe_Pesci "Joe Pesci") was nominated for a [Best Supporting Actor](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actor "Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor") [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Award "Academy Award") for his portrayal of Joey in the 1980 film *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")*, a [biographical film](/wiki/Biographical_film "Biographical film") about his brother Jake's turbulent life and career.

His son, John LaMotta, was an actor and also an amateur boxer who fought in the heavyweight-novice class of the 2001 Golden Gloves championship tournament.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

LaMotta died on December 29, 2020, at age 95.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
